rule_name: SDLT residential standard rates (as implemented by sdlt_engine.py)
summary: Stamp Duty Land Tax on a residential purchase, computed as a progressive 'slice' calculation: 0% on the portion of the price up to £250,000, 5% on the portion from £250,000 to £925,000, 10% on the portion from £925,000 to £1,500,000, and 12% on anything above £1,500,000. The result is rounded down to the whole pound. Prices that are missing, zero or negative are treated as no tax due.
conditions: Live path is calc_v2 via compute_duty; the flag3 parameter is accepted but has no effect (the 3% surcharge branch is dead code)
Negative, zero or missing prices return 0 (mainframe 'no consideration' convention)
Tax is truncated to the whole pound with int()
assumptions: calc_v1 (pre-2014 slab system) and the _vat helper are dead code and were ignored
The disabled _selfcheck expects values consistent with the live band table, so the table appears to be what production actually runs
Thresholds in the _T dict were taken at face value; the code does not indicate which statutory year they correspond to
